file_title: Optimizing In-Store Layout for Bakery Sales Growth
introduction: With the rise in food service sales, there is an opportunity for Lee's Market to review and enhance our in-store layout. This presentation will outline our strategy to optimize product displays and increase bakery sales significantly.

subsections.heading: Current Performance of Bakery Department
subsections.content:
• Analysis shows a potential rise in labor hours by approximately 45 hours weekly per U.S. store due to the proposed bakery layout optimization.
• The increase in labor hours is tied to the addition of new display cases and prep counters, necessitating more frequent restocking and cleaning.
• The adjustment in bakery layout is projected to be fully implemented by the end of Q1 2026.
• Operational feasibility and cost control are key considerations, to ensure the sales gains are not negated by increased staffing costs.
• It's essential to quantify labor shifts to effectively manage the expected increase in workload.
• The increased labor hours are an important factor in our strategy to increase bakery sales by 15% by the end of Q1 2026.
• Further analysis is required to determine the optimal staffing model to handle the increased workload without compromising service quality.
subsections.justification: The content outlines the projected increase in labor hours due to bakery layout optimization, supporting decision-making on staffing and operational planning to meet sales targets.

subsections.heading: Understanding Customer Purchase Behaviour
subsections.content:
• Our loyalty program data indicates that 65% of customers who buy bakery items also purchase coffee
• Peak times for bakery purchases are in the early morning and late afternoon
• About 30% of our customers show a preference for organic bakery items
• Customers typically spend 10-15 minutes in the bakery area
• Repeat customers make up nearly 70% of our bakery sales
subsections.justification: These points focus on customer behavior and preferences, not on how to increase sales by optimizing layout and displays.

subsections.heading: Proposed In-Store Layout and Product Display Adjustments
subsections.content:
• We propose to move the bakery closer to the entrance to draw customers in
• Displaying bakery items near the coffee section could increase impulse buys
• We could test out a ‘Bakery of the Week’ display to highlight different products
• Adjusting the lighting in the bakery area could make the products more appealing
• We propose to increase signage and visibility of bakery items throughout the store
subsections.justification: This slide proposes layout and display adjustments but does not provide any specific strategies to increase bakery sales by 15% by the end of Q1 2026.

conclusion: With the proposed layout and display adjustments, we anticipate a 15% increase in bakery sales by the end of Q1 2026. Let's collaborate on implementing these changes and tracking their impact on our sales performance.
